## reference · ux scanning how users read web

* 79% of users scan web pages, while only 16% read word-by-word.
* Web pages must employ **scannable text** using:
  * Highlighted **keywords**
  * Meaningful **sub-headings**
  * Bulleted **lists**
  * One idea per paragraph
  * Inverted pyramid style, starting with the conclusion
  * Half the word count (or less) than conventional writing
* **Credibility is important** for web users and can be increased by:
  * High-quality graphics
  * Good writing
  * Outbound hypertext links
* Users detest **"marketese"** (promotional writing style with boastful subjective claims).
* **Objective language** improves usability (27% better) and reduces cognitive burden.
* Improved writing styles result in:
  * Concise text: 58% better usability
  * Scannable layout: 47% better usability
  * Combined concise, scannable, and objective: 124% better usability

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/how-users-read-on-the-web/
